title: The Paradox of Choice
subtitle: 为什么选择越多反而越不幸福

paragraphs.en: Modern consumers face an unprecedented abundance of options in nearly every domain of life. Supermarket shelves offer hundreds of cereal varieties, streaming services present thousands of shows, and dating apps display endless potential partners. Psychologists have discovered that this abundance often produces anxiety rather than satisfaction.
paragraphs.zh: 现代消费者在生活的几乎每个领域都面临着前所未有的选择丰富度。超市货架提供数百种谷物品种，流媒体服务呈现数千部节目，而约会应用展示无尽的潜在伴侣。心理学家发现，这种丰富往往产生焦虑而非满足。

paragraphs.en: The paradox of choice describes how excessive options can paralyze decision-making. Faced with too many alternatives, people often struggle to choose at all, experiencing what researchers call decision fatigue. When choices finally occur, dissatisfaction follows as people imagine the roads not taken.
paragraphs.zh: 选择悖论描述了过多的选择如何使决策瘫痪。面对太多的替代选项，人们往往难以做出任何选择，经历研究人员所说的决策疲劳。当最终做出选择时，随之而来的是不满，因为人们会想象那些未走过的路。

paragraphs.en: Research distinguishes between maximizers—those who exhaustively seek the best option—and satisficers—those who accept good enough choices. Maximizers report lower happiness despite often achieving objectively better outcomes. The psychological cost of endless optimization exceeds the benefits of marginally superior selections.
paragraphs.zh: 研究区分了最大化者——那些详尽地寻求最佳选项的人——和满足者——那些接受足够好的选择的人。最大化者报告的幸福感更低，尽管他们通常获得客观上更好的结果。无尽优化的心理成本超过了略微优越选择的收益。

paragraphs.en: Opportunity costs weigh more heavily as options multiply. Each choice means foregoing alternatives that might have been equally good or better. With limited options, we accept trade-offs more easily. Abundant choice makes every decision feel consequential, adding stress to ordinary life choices.
paragraphs.zh: 随着选项增多，机会成本的分量更重。每一个选择都意味着放弃可能同样好或更好的替代选项。选择有限时，我们更容易接受权衡取舍。丰富的选择使每个决定都感觉很重要，为普通的生活选择增添了压力。

paragraphs.en: Strategies exist for navigating choice overload more effectively. Deliberately limiting options, establishing clear criteria before searching, and practicing gratitude for choices made all help manage decision stress. Recognizing that perfect choices rarely exist—and that good enough often truly is—liberates us from exhausting pursuit of optimal outcomes.
paragraphs.zh: 存在更有效地应对选择过载的策略。有意识地限制选项、在搜索前建立明确的标准，以及对已做出的选择练习感恩，都有助于管理决策压力。认识到完美的选择很少存在——而且足够好往往确实就是——将我们从追求最优结果的疲惫中解放出来。
